description:
We are supplying the resources the world needs to help build a better, clearer future. Copper for renewable energy. Potash for sustainable farming. Iron ore and metallurgical coal for the steel needed for global infrastructure and the energy transition. #FutureIsClear

Across our global operations, we are committed to working in ways that are true to our BHP Charter values of Sustainability, Integrity, Respect, Performance, Simplicity and Accountability. Learn more about working at BHP and the exciting career opportunities that exist for professionals, undergraduates and graduates on our website: www.bhp.com.
description_enriched: BHP is a company that aims to bring people and resources together to build a better world. They deliver long-term value and returns through their strategy. They have various businesses and are involved in various sectors such as minerals, commercial, and electric transport.

company_locations_full.location_address: 125 St Georges Ter; Perth, WA 6000, AU
Cerro El Plomo 6000; Santiago, Región Metropolitana 7560623, CL
161 Bay St; 4020; Toronto, Ontario M5J 2T3, CA
Nova South; 160 Victoria Street; London, England SW1E 5LB, GB

company_updates.description:
At the Macquarie Australia Conference, BHP CFO Vandita Pant shared how we’re positioned for the megatrends shaping the global economy - from AI and electrification to food security.

Exposure to these trends is critical for investors. But so is resilience.

At BHP, our portfolio is designed to deliver both - combining long-term growth opportunities with strong, reliable cash flows.

#BHP #Copper #Growth #Potash

Great to host Nine News in Port Hedland this week and give them a first-hand look at some of our new battery-electric #locomotives. 🚆🔋

#Decarbonising our operations isn’t just about putting new technology on the tracks – it’s about building the capability around it. New skills, new infrastructure, and new ways of working.

The #Pilbara is a tough environment and our rail network topography is unique, which is exactly why testing these locomotives here matters. It’s how we ensure this technology performs safely and at scale.

I'm proud of the team-of-teams working together to deliver this - whether that's our Rail and Decarb teams here in Western Australia or our technology partners across the globe - together we are keeping our progress on track!⚡️

BHP Tim Day Ben Gilkison Corey Boreham Dave Kelso Peter Thomas Colin Kerelchuk

Supporting the reduction of greenhouse emissions in the maritime value chain.

Last July, we signed contracts with COSCO Shipping Bulk Co. for the charter of two ammonia dual-fuelled Newcastlemax bulk carriers.

These vessels will be two of only a handful in the world capable of using ammonia as a marine fuel.

This represents an important step towards lowering shipping greenhouse gas emissions in the iron ore seaborne market and moving the maritime sector towards a decarbonised future.

🔗 to learn more in the comments.

#BHP #Maritime #Decarbonisation

“Our partnership with the AFLW has always been about strengthening communities through connection, inclusion and opportunity, on and off the field." - Geraldine Slattery, Australia President, BHP.

We’re pleased to continue our partnership and shared commitment with AFL - Australian Football League to strengthen communities and support the growth and development of women’s football. 

The partnership reflects a shared ambition to drive meaningful social outcomes, both within corporate environments and across regional communities where BHP operates. 

🔗 in the comments to learn more.

#BHP #AFLW #Partnership

When Shirley moved to Chile, she didn’t just start a new job - she found her place in mining.

Today, she’s a warehouse operator at Spence, building her career with BHP.

As a member of an Indigenous community, Shirley says our inclusive culture has given her the confidence to grow - bringing safety, respect and personal development into every part of her life.

Her journey shows what’s possible when people feel seen, supported and empowered to succeed.

#BHP #Mining #Inclusion #Diversity

CEO Elect Brandon Craig spoke at the annual Bank of America Global Metals, Mining and Steel Conference this week. He told investors:

✅ He was excited to lead BHP as CEO from July 1. 

✅ BHP is operating world-class tier 1 assets with excellence.

✅ BHP is the world’s largest copper producer and has increased copper production guidance for the next two years at a time when supply across the sector remains constrained.

Link in comments to read more.

#BHP #growth #leadership

As the world transitions to a lower-carbon future, copper will play an increasingly vital role in the technologies and infrastructure that support the energy transition - such as electric vehicles and renewable energy systems, including wind turbines, solar panels and battery storage.

We produce resources that are essential to this transition, and we are committed to reducing the greenhouse gas emissions generated from their production.

🔗 to learn more in the comments.

#BHP #Copper #Decarbonisation

At our Jansen mine in Saskatoon, we are redefining what's possible underground.

We made two 8.5m wide and 34m high excavations for our raw ore bin, removing more than 5,500 tonnes of rock safely and efficiently. Completed in just 24 days, this industry-first approach delivered a ten-storey opening from the bottom up, significantly faster and safer than traditional methods.

The Potash from our Jansen mine is expected to produce approximately 10 per cent of the world’s demand by 2031.

#Jansen #Innovation #Potash

Creating jobs. Building skills. Supporting communities.

BHP’s total direct economic contribution for FY2025 was US$46.8 billion. 

This includes payments to suppliers, wages and benefits for over 90,000 employees and contractors, dividends, taxes, royalties and voluntary investment in social projects across the communities where we operate.

The world needs more of the materials we produce to develop, decarbonise and digitalise. We remain well positioned to sustainably meet this demand and create long-term value for our shareholders, local communities, customers, suppliers and partners.

Link to BHP's Economic Contribution Report 2025 in the comments.

#BHP #Mining #Contribution

There is an engine of improvement that revs faster and faster each day at BHP. 

It’s fuelled by the ingenuity of our people, and I’m excited to see how much faster our teams can make it go with the help of AI in the years to come. 

I saw this in action on my recent return to our Integrated Remote Operations Centre in Perth. 

The team is using computer vision and AI to reduce the need for unplanned maintenance, improve our product quality, and keep our iron ore flowing to customers. 

The AI scans the more than 300 tonnes of material that move along the conveyor belt every minute looking for oversized rocks or contaminants. It quickly and accurately detects material that can cause serious damage to our equipment of affect product quality landing at the port.

Once detected, our operators in Perth can trigger a 15-minute pause and work with our site team to safely remove any contaminants – avoiding tears in the belts that have been known to shut them down for up to three days.

This is a simple but powerful system that delivers clear benefits on safety, productivity and quality. 

It highlights the immense potential of AI to help our teams accelerate the rate of improvement across every part of our supply chain. 

And it’s another way our Western Australian Iron Ore team contributes to BHP’s relentless drive for operating excellence. 

Thank you to Ryan and Renee for taking me through it.

Port General Manager Dan Mossman and his marine team oversee one of the world’s most complex channel systems. 🚢 

In Western Australia, our port operations handle nearly 2,500 vessel movements each year. Through a clear focus on simplification, capability development and commercial rigour, Dan and his team are driving strong production gains and cost efficiencies. 

Over the past nine months, BHP has achieved record iron ore production at Western Australia Iron Ore (WAIO). Our iron ore business includes integrated iron ore mines, rail and port operations in the Pilbara.

#BHP #Leadership #IronOre

As Escondida marks its 35th anniversary, we recognise the career of Luis Chivano, Senior Maintenance Engineer, whose 13‑year journey with the operation came to a close this year - and whose legacy continues through the next generation.

Luis’s son, Alonso Chivano, now works as a Project Engineer in Integrated Engineering, contributing to the same operation that shaped his father’s career.

“It’s a source of great pride to see my son continue this path,” says Luis. For Alonso, following in his father’s footsteps is both an inspiration and a responsibility.

At Escondida, we have spent 35 years delivering the copper the world needs - while building careers, connections and purpose that last across generations.

#Escondida35 #Copper #Chile

Progress in the mining sector depends on continuously improving how we discover, develop and operate assets.

At BHP, our people are driving that progress - using data, AI and automation to make everyday work safer, smarter and more efficient.

Learn more: https://bhp.co/SB

#Mining #AI #BHP

The machines are big. The shifts are long. And the learning curve? Even steeper.

Meet the apprentices getting hands-on experience in Queensland.

On site, surrounded by draglines, shovels and systems that stretch for miles, they’re not just working - they’re building skills, confidence and epic careers in mining.

Hear their stories first-hand 👉https://bhp.co/Sy

#BHP #Apprentices #Queensland

Lasting community outcomes start with investment in the right areas. 

Through the Western Australian Government’s Resources Community Investment Initiative, BHP is committing $50 million to boost housing for essential government workers in the Pilbara region of Western Australia. 

This investment will supply Port Hedland with more homes, supporting teachers, nurses, police and other frontline workers who help our communities grow and thrive every day. 

“Strong Pilbara communities start with people having access to safe, affordable housing.” - Tim Day, Asset President Western Australia Iron Ore 

Better housing means more resilient communities, improved services, and stronger local economies – ensuring that these communities are great places to live and work. 

That’s how social investment creates shared value. 

#BHP #Community #SocialInvestment

One million tonnes. One significant copper milestone. 🎉

Carrapateena in South Australia has officially produced one million tonnes of copper concentrate since production began in December 2019, reinforcing the strength of BHP’s copper portfolio as global demand for copper continues to rise.

To put that scale into perspective, it’s equivalent to:
📦 33,000 shipping containers  
🏊 200 Olympic swimming pools  
🌉 19 Sydney Harbour Bridges

This milestone reflects the dedication of the Copper South Australia team and the role copper will play in supporting electrification, decarbonisation, and long-term economic growth.

#Copper #Growth #BHP

At the World Copper Conference 2026 in Santiago, BHP highlighted Chile’s critical role in meeting the world’s future copper demand.

Alejandro Tapia, President of Escondida, spoke to the importance of copper in enabling electrification, decarbonisation and digitalisation - and the need for certainty to unlock long-term investment.

With copper demand expected to grow ~70% by 2050, BHP is focused on operational excellence, disciplined investment and progressing future growth in Chile and beyond.

#Copper #Growth #BHP #WorldCopperConference

🌱 In New South Wales, Australia we are looking to the future ahead of the planned closure of our Mt Arthur Coal mine by 2030.

We recently opened the doors to our site and welcomed the local Hunter Valley community for our annual Community Tree Planting Day, where 1,000 trees were planted along a future wildlife corridor. 

We know there's a lot of questions about the planned closure of Mt Arthur Coal, follow the link in the comments below to find out about how we are powering what's next.

#PoweringWhatsNext #BHP

In this edition, learn more about Chile’s role in meeting the world’s growing copper needs, strong operational performance highlighted in our latest review, the real-world impact of community investment, and our innovative partnerships in China.

Alejandro Tapia Godoy, Karina Gistelinck, Jessica Farrell

#BHP #Copper #Growth

Today, we released our Operational Review for the nine months ended 31 March 2026. 

We delivered strong performance over the past nine months, achieving operational records at Escondida, Copper SA and WAIO. 

Our balance sheet remains strong, and we have a significant pipeline of copper and potash growth projects, to deliver long term value through the cycle. 

Link to learn more in the comments. 

#BHP #OperationalReview #CopperGrowth

🌱 In New South Wales, Australia we are looking to the future ahead of the planned closure of our Mt Arthur Coal mine by 2030.

We recently opened the doors to our site and welcomed the local Hunter Valley community for our annual Community Tree Planting Day, where 1,000 trees were planted along a future wildlife corridor. 

We know there's a lot of questions about the planned closure of Mt Arthur Coal, follow the link in the comments below to find out about how we are powering what's next.

#PoweringWhatsNext #BHP

In this edition, learn more about Chile’s role in meeting the world’s growing copper needs, strong operational performance highlighted in our latest review, the real-world impact of community investment, and our innovative partnerships in China.

Alejandro Tapia Godoy, Karina Gistelinck, Jessica Farrell

#BHP #Copper #Growth

Today, we released our Operational Review for the nine months ended 31 March 2026. 

We delivered strong performance over the past nine months, achieving operational records at Escondida, Copper SA and WAIO. 

Our balance sheet remains strong, and we have a significant pipeline of copper and potash growth projects, to deliver long term value through the cycle. 

Link to learn more in the comments. 

#BHP #OperationalReview #CopperGrowth

Meet Carla and Bryan, members of BHP Chile’s Graduate Program at Spence. After navigating a highly competitive selection process, they are now taking their first steps in an environment that fosters development, collaboration, and learning.

For Carla, this experience is about building her career and continuing to grow through commitment and effort. For Bryan, being part of the program reinforces that inclusion is real - he’s working in a place where he can develop with confidence and truly be himself.

Their stories demonstrate how diverse teams and different perspectives contribute to shaping a more inclusive mining industry, ready for the future.

#Chile #Mining #Inclusion

“It all starts with geology, both in the discovery of an ore body and in the mining,” says Mitch, BHP Principal Geoscientist.

Broken Hill, New South Wales, Australia, is where BHP’s story began more than 140 years ago, and the geological lessons learned here helped shape how ore bodies are understood today.

That same thinking now underpins BHP’s global portfolio. As Mitch puts it, "World-class geoscience is critical to the success of our operations and future growth."

As the world changes, so does what we deliver. We focus on the resources that matter most now and for the years ahead. Copper to keep the world connected, iron ore and coal to make the steel that builds it and potash, in the future, to help feed it.

#BHP #Exploration #Geology

Our team in Canada recently marked a significant milestone with the final truss installation at our potash product storage building - one month ahead of schedule.

🌱 Potash is a key ingredient in fertiliser, essential for improving crop yields and supporting global food security.

This achievement completes the structural framework of this large-scale facility, which will store Jansen potash prior to loading and shipment to customers around the world.

Jansen is BHP's potash mine in Saskatchewan, Canada, with first production scheduled for 2027.
  
#BHP #Potash #Canada

It's a family affair!

Most families catch up around the dinner table. The Filbey's do it in high‑vis and steel caps. 👷♂️👷♀️

At BHP’s Perth Rebuild Centre, in Western Australia, Leon Filbey works alongside three of his sons - Brad, Dean and Adam - each carving out their own path across operations, leadership and technical excellence. From rebuilding critical components to leading teams and driving reliability, it’s a family united by pride in their work.

And it doesn’t stop there. Mum Dorothy is also part of the BHP family, guiding the next generation of trades as a Maintenance Supervisor at Mining Area C.

Different roles. Shared values. A lot of Filbeys.

📸Dean, (Dad) Leon, Brad, Adam

What will it take to meet the world’s growing demand for critical minerals while operating more safely and efficiently? 

Increasingly, the answer lies in how effectively the industry applies data, artificial intelligence and digital technologies to improve decisions. 

In our latest BHP Insights piece, BHP Digital Officer Mikko Tepponen shared some insight into how these tools are helping connect teams, data and operations across the mining value chain.

Link to learn more in the comments.

#BHP #AI #Insights

At 1,331m deep, this new shaft is part of our mine expansion at Prominent Hill in South Australia. 

Once complete, it will extend the life of the mine to at least 2040, open access to deeper parts of the ore body, and increase production.

Prominent Hill produces one of the highest grades of copper concentrate in the world. With copper demand expected to grow 70% by 2050, copper is a big and growing part of our business.

This image was taken during the sinking stage breakthrough to the mid shaft area, approximately 650m below surface.

#Copper #Growth #BHP

📸 Credit: Daniel Lagacé

For 35 years, Escondida has helped lead the world in copper - and in transforming mining from within.

As part of Escondida’s 35th anniversary, we’re sharing the story of Karen Toro, a Quality Specialist who joined the operation in 1997.

Karen has seen first-hand how mining has evolved. Today, women make up more than 45% of Escondida’s workforce. That progress reflects the efforts of those who paved the way, and our ongoing commitment to building a stronger, more inclusive industry for the future.

Because diversity strengthens how we think, how we work and how we lead - and it continues to shape what comes next.

#Escondida35 #Mining #Inclusion

What will it take to meet the world’s growing demand for critical minerals while operating more safely and efficiently? 

Increasingly, the answer lies in how effectively the industry applies data, artificial intelligence and digital technologies to improve decisions. 

In our latest BHP Insights piece, BHP Digital Officer Mikko Tepponen shared some insight into how these tools are helping connect teams, data and operations across the mining value chain.

Link to learn more in the comments.

#BHP #AI #Insights

At 1,331m deep, this new shaft is part of our mine expansion at Prominent Hill in South Australia. 

Once complete, it will extend the life of the mine to at least 2040, open access to deeper parts of the ore body, and increase production.

Prominent Hill produces one of the highest grades of copper concentrate in the world. With copper demand expected to grow 70% by 2050, copper is a big and growing part of our business.

This image was taken during the sinking stage breakthrough to the mid shaft area, approximately 650m below surface.

#Copper #Growth #BHP

📸 Credit: Daniel Lagacé

For 35 years, Escondida has helped lead the world in copper - and in transforming mining from within.

As part of Escondida’s 35th anniversary, we’re sharing the story of Karen Toro, a Quality Specialist who joined the operation in 1997.

Karen has seen first-hand how mining has evolved. Today, women make up more than 45% of Escondida’s workforce. That progress reflects the efforts of those who paved the way, and our ongoing commitment to building a stronger, more inclusive industry for the future.

Because diversity strengthens how we think, how we work and how we lead - and it continues to shape what comes next.

#Escondida35 #Mining #Inclusion

This is how we load our trains with iron ore.

Nicole, who is a Process Technician at our South Flank operations in Western Australia, takes us through the many steps involved in getting iron ore from our stockpiles to the ships in Port Hedland.

#SupplyChain #Trains #BHP

This week in Canberra, CEO Mike Henry met with Australian Government representatives, including the Federal Resources Minister Madeleine King and Federal Trade Minister Don Farrell, alongside incoming BHP CEO Brandon Craig.

For over 140 years, BHP has contributed to Australia’s economic development, trade relationships and national prosperity. We are committed to building on this legacy by working constructively with governments and partners across the country.

The discussions provided a valuable opportunity to engage on the growing global interest in critical minerals, and the role Australia can play in meeting future demand.

#BHP #Mining #Contribution

Meet Lucas. 🚲👷🏼

By day, Lucas helps keep one of the world’s largest underground operations running smoothly. Off swing, you’ll find him cycling the Adelaide Hills in South Australia.

Lucas is Superintendent, Control Operations at Olympic Dam. 

Based in the Mine Control Operations room - known as “the Tower” - Lucas and his team plan and coordinate shift‑by‑shift activity across our 24/7 operation, keeping people, equipment and priorities aligned underground.

We are investing over A$840 million in a series of growth-enabling projects at Olympic Dam, to strengthen the foundations of underground mining productivity and continue building its world-class copper province in the far north of South Australia.

#Copper #Growth #SouthAustralia

Family Day from Goonyella, Queensland 📸

Across two huge days, almost 400 of our BMA team members and their families turned out to tour the Goonyella workshop and lookout, get up close with our equipment, and jump into plenty of family fun competitions and activities.

#FamilyDay #Goonyella #BHP

In this edition, learn more about our CEO transition, how AI is strengthening decision-making, the real impact of community investment, and celebrate 35 years of copper mining in Chile.

#BHP #Leadership #AI #CopperGrowth

As Escondida marks 35 years, we’re sharing the story of Marco López Olivares, Cathodes Maintenance Manager - and a career shaped by family and commitment.

Marco’s connection to Escondida began long before his own career. His mother was part of the early Human Resources team, while his father built a 25‑year career at the Los Colorados project, defined by dedication and hard work.

Today, Marco marks 20 years at BHP and has been part of Escondida since 2015 - carrying a legacy across generations.

“If my daughters also choose this path, they will have my full support.”

After 35 years, we celebrate the people and families who continue to shape Escondida’s future.

#Escondida35 #Mining #BHP

Meet Corey from our Operational Decarbonisation team, working out of Port Hedland, Western Australia.

Corey takes us behind the scenes of Australia’s first purpose-built, heavy-haul battery-electric locomotives. We’re trialling these new machines to help us understand what the future of our rail operations could look like⚡🔋🚂 


#BHP #RailwaysofthePilbara #Mining

Escondida is making significant progress towards its future. 🎉

This week, Escondida submitted an application to Chile’s Environmental Evaluation System for the construction of a new concentrator plant, representing an investment exceeding US$5 billion.

The proposed concentrator is intended to replace the processing capacity of Los Colorados as it nears the end of its operational life and is a crucial component of Escondida’s long-term strategy to maintain operations for many years ahead.

We were honored to host the President of Chile, José Antonio Kast, at our CEIM mining training centre in Antofagasta, where he highlighted the investment.

As the largest copper producer in the world, BHP has a clear trajectory for growth and is well-positioned to create value in the global copper market, both now and in the future.

#BHP #Investment #Copper

Rehabilitation at Mt Arthur is not a future activity - it’s happening every year. 🪴🪴🪴

Across our Mt Arthur operation in New South Wales, we rehabilitate around 100 hectares of active mining land annually, planting approximately 10,000 trees each year as part of our ongoing commitment to restoring the landscape.

That commitment extends beyond our teams on site. At our first Community Tree Planting Day last year, local school students from Muswellbrook and Scone helped plant more than 1,000 trees on rehabilitated land - contributing directly to the next chapter of the site.

Through progressive rehabilitation, Mt Arthur Coal continues to restore land as mining advances, while also supporting broader regional biodiversity outcomes through thoughtful rehabilitation design and planting activities that will continue year on year.

#BHP #Rehabilitation #Mining

BHP announces new CEO.

BHP’s Board of Directors has appointed Brandon Craig to succeed Mike Henry from 1 July 2026. 

Mike Henry has been an outstanding CEO for BHP. Under his leadership, BHP has transformed into a safer and more productive company, financially strong with a more resilient and growth-oriented portfolio.

Learn more: https://lnkd.in/gEc9PMEX

#BHP #CEO #Leadership

Goodbye to our Queensland summer interns 👋

We've farewelled our BMA summer interns after they spent the last few months gaining firsthand experience at the coal face. 

We extend our thanks to them for their curiosity and enthusiasm throughout their time with us.

Applications for BHP's Australian Graduate and Intern Programs are now open. 

Link in the comments to find out more. 👇 

#BHP #Queensland

✨ 35 years ago, a discovery changed the future of copper.

What began as a geological finding in the Atacama Desert has grown into the world’s largest copper-producing operation.

Escondida was built on a foundation of vision, innovation, and the people who took the very first steps - including the first blast of the orebody, a moment few imagined would mark the beginning of something so significant.

Discover the story of this beginning through the voices of Luis and Hugo, who were part of the operation from its earliest days.

Today, the Escondida team combines advanced technologies, a fully integrated value chain, and operations at an unprecedented scale in mining to produce the copper that powers the energy transition and modern life.

This reel shows where it all began - and the journey that continues.

#Chile #Copper #Escondida35

As the world’s largest copper producer, we are well positioned today and have a clear pathway to significantly grow our production.

With global copper demand projected to rise by 70% by 2050, our commitment to growth is stronger than ever.

We remain focused on operating safely, investing with discipline and creating long-term value for shareholders, communities and partners.

Learn more: https://bhp.co/Sc

#BHP #Copper #Growth

At our Jansen Potash Project in Canada, safety leadership is built from the ground up.

For Mickey, one of our underground miners, safety starts with staying constantly aware of her environment and identifying potential hazards before they become risks. It’s a mindset of accountability and care that she’s committed to passing on to others.

As she puts it, her goal is to help her teammates “be aware of their surroundings and be safe at work.”

This is how safety culture grows - through people who lead by example, every single day.

#Safety #Mining #BHP

Ever wondered what it takes to move our iron ore from pit to port? 

Five mines ✅ 
Four processing hubs ✅ 
More than 1,300 km of rail and port infrastructure ✅ 
One Integrated Remote Operations Centre (IROC) ✅ 
And of course, our people ✅ 

🎥 Take a look behind the scenes with our Western Australia Iron Ore IROC team.

Attention university students! Applications for BHP's Australian Graduate and Intern Programs are now open.

🟠 Work on real projects that make a big impact on the future.
🟠 Learn from skilled industry experts and build the tools you need for a successful career.
🟠 Grow future ready skills in engineering, tech, science, business, build confidence on-site and see how our operations come to life.

Whether you are chasing a Summer Internship or a permanent Graduate role, you’ll be supported with training, mentors and a connected cohort of interns and grads who grow together.  

Applications are open until 6 April 2026 – apply now and start shaping your future with BHP today.

At The Australian Financial Review Business Summit, BHP Chair Ross McEwan spoke about the shifting global environment and the importance of staying competitive in a world defined by increasing contest - for capital, capability and influence.

In Australia, we have natural advantages. Realising them will require government and business working together to lift productivity, unlock approvals for nationally significant projects, deliver affordable and reliable energy, accelerate adoption of automation and AI, and build the skills needed for the jobs of the future.

We want to be part of the solution - and we’re optimistic about what can be achieved when we act with pace and partnership.

#AFRBusinessSummit #Productivity #Competitiveness

Ever wondered what it takes to move our iron ore from pit to port? 

Five mines ✅ 
Four processing hubs ✅ 
More than 1,300 km of rail and port infrastructure ✅ 
One Integrated Remote Operations Centre (IROC) ✅ 
And of course, our people ✅ 

🎥 Take a look behind the scenes with our Western Australia Iron Ore IROC team.

Attention university students! Applications for BHP's Australian Graduate and Intern Programs are now open.

🟠 Work on real projects that make a big impact on the future.
🟠 Learn from skilled industry experts and build the tools you need for a successful career.
🟠 Grow future ready skills in engineering, tech, science, business, build confidence on-site and see how our operations come to life.

Whether you are chasing a Summer Internship or a permanent Graduate role, you’ll be supported with training, mentors and a connected cohort of interns and grads who grow together.  

Applications are open until 6 April 2026 – apply now and start shaping your future with BHP today.

In Australia, we have natural advantages. Realising them will require government and business working together to lift productivity, unlock approvals for nationally significant projects, deliver affordable and reliable energy, accelerate adoption of automation and AI, and build the skills needed for the jobs of the future.

We want to be part of the solution - and we’re optimistic about what can be achieved when we act with pace and partnership.

#AFRBusinessSummit #Productivity #Competitiveness

Construction of a sixth car dumper is officially underway at our Nelson Point iron ore port operations in Western Australia.

This is one of our biggest port projects to date, unlocking capacity, boosting productivity, and improving supply chain resilience - with a massive team effort behind it.

#Investment #BHP
We have a deliberately diversified portfolio, designed to deliver returns and growth now and into the future.
company_updates.reshared_post_description:
We recently announced really strong half year results, and it was far from a once off.

As I discussed with Morgan L Brennan from CNBC, it was yet another period of consistent execution – operationally, financially and strategically. 

We remain focused on running our base business better and better every year, and unlocking the attractive growth options we have in front of us.
 
We have a deliberately diversified portfolio, designed to deliver returns and growth now and into the future. 

In the four years to 2025, we grew copper production by around 30 per cent. 

And we have some of the best growth options of any miner - in Chile, Argentina, Australia and the United States - to build on our position as the world’s largest copper producer.

Those growth options sit alongside our entry into a new business in potash, which is expected to deliver first production in mid-2027. 

It’s a great position for BHP and our shareholders – both for now and for decades to come.

company_updates.description:
🔋 Our second set of battery-electric locomotives from Progress Rail, A Caterpillar Company are freshly painted and getting ready to make the journey from Brazil to Port Hedland, Western Australia. 

Once they arrive, BHP will be the only mining company in the world trialling two different mainline heavy‑haul battery-electric locomotive technologies and suppliers side‑by‑side. 

#Decarbonisation #BHP

"Financially, BHP is in fantastic shape. We’re consistently executing our strategy with a high-quality portfolio leveraged to megatrends, tier-one assets, and a track record of operational excellence.

These factors are allowing us to advance our growth pipeline in copper and potash while creating value for the world around us." - Vandita Pant, CFO.

For deeper insight into our half-year results and growth outlook, watch our In-Conversation with Vandita and CEO Mike Henry. Link in the comments.

#Copper #Mining #Performance

In case you missed it, our half year results are out - now for the conversation behind them.
 
In our latest In Conversation, Mike Henry, CEO, and Vandita Pant, CFO, discuss what the results tell us and the themes shaping the road ahead.

🎧 Tune in - link in the comments
 
#Mining #InConversation #Growth

Our half-year 2026 results reflect strong operational and financial performance, underpinned by a clear focus on safety, disciplined execution and growth in future-facing commodities.

This period marked a significant milestone for BHP, with copper contributing more than half of group earnings for the first time. Strong production, cost discipline and operational reliability across our portfolio supported record earnings and reinforced the strength of our strategy.

As the world’s largest copper producer, we are well positioned today and have a clear pathway to significantly grow our production.

We remain focused on operating safely, investing with discipline and creating long-term value for shareholders, communities and partners.

#BHP #Performance #Copper

In this edition, hear direct insights on our strategy, performance and outlook from our CEO, learn how we're unlocking value from our portfolio in new ways, and meet the next generation of mining talent in Chile. 

#BHP #Mining #Performance

In this edition, hear direct insights on our strategy, performance and outlook from our CEO, learn how we're unlocking value from our portfolio in new ways, and meet the next generation of mining talent in Chile. 

#BHP #Mining #Performance

It’s been a strong first half across BHP.

We’re well positioned in the right commodities, with copper delivering more than 50% of group earnings for the first time, and Western Australia Iron Ore (WAIO) achieving record first half production and shipments.

Strong operational performance and financial discipline are allowing us to invest in our assets and future growth.

For deeper insight into our half‑year results, watch our In‑Conversation. Link in the comments.

#Copper #Mining #Performance

Exciting news at Escondida! 🎓🚀 

We are thrilled to welcome 35 talented interns and thesis students who are embarking on their professional journeys with various teams at our Escondida operations in Chile. 

This new cohort showcases the promising talent from the Antofagasta Region, and we can't wait to see the valuable contributions they will make to our operations. 

We wish each of them a fulfilling experience filled with learning, challenges, and opportunities. 

#Chile #BHP #Education
BHP's Western Australian Iron Ore business has achieved record production and shipments for the first half of FY26 - a feat only possible by having exceptional people.

Meet Sarina, a Senior Development Metallurgist at Olympic Dam, South Australia. 👷🏼

Sarina helps shape the future of the Olympic Dam concentrator by forecasting performance and enhancing safety and efficiency. 

Combining data analysis with hands-on experience, she ensures that improvements benefit daily operations. Passionate about leaving things better for people, processes, and the environment, she also chairs BHP Women in South Australia, promoting positive change. 💪

We are investing over A$840 million in a series of growth-enabling projects at Olympic Dam, to strengthen the foundations of underground mining productivity and continue building our world-class copper province in the far north of South Australia.

#BHP #Mining #Copper

"My favorite thing about working here at Olympic Dam is the culture."  says Molly, who recently transitioned from a Continuous Improvement Specialist role to Supervisor Villages. 

Whether you're considering a career change or simply curious about life on site, there are countless roles, pathways, and people shaping the future of the industry.

For Molly, it's the culture, teamwork, and inclusivity that make our Olympic Dam operations in South Australia a fantastic place to work.

As one of the world's most significant copper deposits, Olympic Dam is poised to play a crucial role in meeting the projected 70% increase in global demand for copper by 2050.

#BHP #Mining #Careers #Copper

We are deeply committed to creating positive outcomes for the communities in which we operate.

In Queensland, we take great pride in our enduring relationship with the region. Our initiatives have focused on supporting education and skills development at every level, providing grants for local events and initiatives, and investing in programs and partnerships that strengthen and enrich our communities. These efforts reflect our connection to the area and our confidence in its future.

The BHP and BMA FY2025 Community Contribution Report for Queensland showcases authentic stories and the tangible benefits that our initiatives have brought to local communities.

Read more about how we are helping to build a better world from Queensland 👉https://bhp.co/RT

#BHP #Community #Contribution

We’re pleased to announce the BHP Xplor 2026 cohort, the program’s largest and most diverse group to date.

Now in its fourth year, Xplor brings together early-stage exploration companies, geoscience organizations, and technology teams from across the global exploration ecosystem. This year’s cohort reflects a more connected view of discovery, where geological insight, data, science, and technology increasingly intersect.

We’re looking forward to working with the 2026 cohort and learning alongside them as we explore what discovery could look like next.

Learn more about BHP Xplor: https://bhp.co/RP

#BHP #Xplor #Innovation

Artificial intelligence is increasingly shaping how BHP operates - supporting safer, more reliable and more productive mining as demand for critical minerals continues to grow. 

From exploration through to processing and logistics, AI is moving beyond experimentation and becoming part of day-to-day operations across our global portfolio, supported by strong governance and clear accountability. 

Learn more here: https://bhp.co/RO

#ArtificialIntelligence #Mining #BHP

🤖 What is a Robotic Cathode Stripping Machine?

Brett is the Superintendent of the Production Refinery at our Olympic Dam copper mine in South Australia and has helped lead the charge in making copper refining safer, more accessible and autonomous.

Cathode stripping is a crucial step in the copper refining process, involving the removal of thin sheets of nearly pure copper (known as cathodes) from large metal plates after refining.

At Olympic Dam, Brett has helped lead a transformation in copper refining with a fully automated Robotic Cathode Stripping Machine. 

Working with teams across projects, engineering and execution, this innovation strips and processes cathodes without exposing operators to potential hazards, boosts reliability and cuts downtime caused by copper jams. 

Their game-changing work was recognised at BHP’s Health, Safety, Environment and Community (HSEC) Awards – celebrating the people who make a difference.

"My favorite thing about working here at Olympic Dam is the culture."  says Molly, who recently transitioned from a Continuous Improvement Specialist role to Supervisor Villages. 

Whether you're considering a career change or simply curious about life on site, there are countless roles, pathways, and people shaping the future of the industry.

For Molly, it's the culture, teamwork, and inclusivity that make our Olympic Dam operations in South Australia a fantastic place to work.

As one of the world's most significant copper deposits, Olympic Dam is poised to play a crucial role in meeting the projected 70% increase in global demand for copper by 2050.

Learn more here: https://bhp.co/RO

#ArtificialIntelligence #Mining #BHP

🤖 What is a Robotic Cathode Stripping Machine?

Brett is the Superintendent of the Production Refinery at our Olympic Dam copper mine in South Australia and has helped lead the charge in making copper refining safer, more accessible and autonomous.

Cathode stripping is a crucial step in the copper refining process, involving the removal of thin sheets of nearly pure copper (known as cathodes) from large metal plates after refining.

At Olympic Dam, Brett has helped lead a transformation in copper refining with a fully automated Robotic Cathode Stripping Machine. 

Working with teams across projects, engineering and execution, this innovation strips and processes cathodes without exposing operators to potential hazards, boosts reliability and cuts downtime caused by copper jams. 

Their game-changing work was recognised at BHP’s Health, Safety, Environment and Community (HSEC) Awards – celebrating the people who make a difference.

Autonomous haulage is all about safety. Our Mining Area C iron ore site in Western Australia is BHP's eighth global autonomous haulage deployment.

It includes our third-largest autonomous operating zone, connecting 30 trucks and five diggers. At the heart of it all are our people, driving safer operations, better performance, and a smarter future.

#BHP #Autonomous #Mining

As global demand for copper continues to rise, we’re making strong progress in lowering the greenhouse gas emissions intensity of our copper operations in South Australia. Last year, we entered into our third and largest renewable electricity supply agreement for Copper SA.

“The benefits will go beyond our Copper SA operations as the project will contribute to a more resilient energy future for South Australia through new infrastructure that supports regional communities and creates jobs.” - Anna Wiley, Asset President, Copper South Australia, BHP

Through expanded renewable electricity supply and ongoing decarbonisation initiatives, we’re working to produce the copper the world needs - more sustainably and responsibly.

Learn more: https://bhp.co/RM

#BHP #Decarbonisation #SouthAustralia

"We come to work with a smile, we leave with a smile, and we're safe while we do it."  

At our South Flank operations in Western Australia, safety and performance are always top of mind. Just ask Shaq, Planned Maintenance Supervisor, who has successfully advanced from being on the tools to his current leadership position at the MEM workshop.

Opened in 2021, South Flank is Australia's biggest new mine in decades, combining with Mining Area C to form the world's largest iron ore hub.

#BHP #IronOre #Teamwork

🏆 Award-winning land rehabilitation and management at Mt Arthur Coal 🏆

Meet Jimmy, Superintendent of Environment at Mt Arthur Coal in New South Wales. Jimmy and the team at Mt Arthur Coal have continued to deliver on our commitment to leaving a positive environmental legacy.

Together with Robson Civil Projects, they’ve delivered one of the state’s most significant progressive rehabilitation programs - spanning earthworks, drainage, topsoil improvement, seeding, weed/pest control, and biodiversity restoration.

The project's achievements were recognised recently, with Robson Civil Projects winning the Engineering Success of the Year Award at the 2025 Australian Mining Prospects Award.

Learn more: https://bhp.co/Rl

#BHP #Rehabilitation #PoweringWhatsNext

Today, we released our Operational Review for the half-year ended 31 December 2025.

We delivered another half of strong performance, achieving new operational records across our copper and iron ore assets.

As we head into H2 FY26, we’re building on solid operating momentum and continuing to invest for the decade ahead, supported by a significant copper growth pipeline.

Link to learn more in the comments.

#BHP #OperationalReview #CopperGrowth

#BHP #OperationalReview #CopperGrowth

At the International Economic Forum of the Americas, BHP CEO Mike Henry outlined the critical role copper plays in the global economy, as the world accelerates towards decarbonisation and digitalisation. 

As global demand for copper is set to rise by 70% by 2050, our commitment to growth is stronger than ever.

Watch the full presentation here 👉 https://bhp.co/Ra

Brodie's WOW Moment!

Introducing Brodie, the Casting Production Superintendent at our Olympic Dam operations in South Australia. In this spotlight, she reveals what inspires her in her role: teamwork!

Copper South Australia employs a workforce of approximately 8,000 individuals, including onsite contractors, and last financial year, it produced 316,000 tonnes of copper.

The global demand for copper is expected to surge by 70% by 2050, fueled by population growth, increasing living standards, and the transition to renewable energy. This trend presents a unique generational opportunity for South Australia, which is home to two-thirds of Australia’s copper resources - some of the finest in the world.

#BHP #CopperGrowth #SouthAustralia

In this edition, we share our CEO's insights on copper demand, and our future growth plans, celebrate an industry-first autonomy milestone, and hear from a change champion in Chile.

Subscribe to keep up to date with the latest news and insights from BHP.

#BHP #CopperGrowth #Mining

We’re proud to work alongside local organisations that are making a real difference in the communities where we operate.

Together with Ear Science Institute Australia and Puntukurnu Aboriginal Medical Service, we are helping to improve ear health in remote communities near our Pilbara operations in Western Australia.

Surgical Fast Track means children like nine-year-old Sonny can access quality care in Port Hedland and return home within three days.

The initiative operates by chartering flights from remote locations, facilitating quick surgical access and support from local health staff.

#BHP #CommunityPartnerships #Pilbara

"It’s the community that makes life here. It doesn't matter if you are a cleaner, truck driver, bus driver, mining manager, or in business support; everyone eats dinner together and shares stories."

Meet Sam, a mother of three and an underground truck driver at Olympic Dam. When Sam is not enjoying a sunset (and not cooking!) on-site, she's embracing family time at the beach.

“It’s quite the ‘sea change’ every other week as I transition from the desert back to the sea! The lifestyle is fantastic, and I love the contrast. I take pride in the work we do at Olympic Dam and in being part of this team.”

#BHP #Mining #SouthAustralia

Wrapped up for winter! See how we enclosed our mill buildings to keep construction going in Canada's sub-zero temperatures. 

Saskatchewan is home to Jansen, our Potash mine, which is on track for first production in 2027.

Family Day at Escondida!

An unforgettable visit that offered a glimpse into the world of mining at Escondida, our Chilean operation and the largest copper producer globally.

The highlight of the day? A fun twist where parents interviewed their children about their experiences! Their reactions spoke volumes:

✨ “It’s HUGE!”  
✨ “I never imagined this was your workplace.”  
✨ “I’m so proud of you.”  

From the SAG mills at Laguna Seca to the CAEX truck at the Maintenance Workshop and the stunning view of the Escondida pit in action - it was an unforgettable day!

#BHP #Mining #Copper

Over the next 25 years, we expect demand for copper to increase by almost 70%.

The growth in demand is driven by development in emerging economies, electrification, AI and data centres - and BHP is ready to play its part in meeting that demand.

Our copper South Australia asset is a world-class copper province in the far north of South Australia. Copper SA has three underground mines at Olympic Dam, Prominent Hill and Carrapateena, which feed a centralised smelter and refinery at Olympic Dam. There is also an exploration project at Oak Dam, which could be a future fourth mine in the province.

#CopperGrowth #BHP #SouthAustralia

What a rig!

Rig 36 is one of Ausdrill’s exploration rigs operating at BMA's Caval Ridge Mine in Queensland.

This impressive rig has industry-leading technology, including automated rod operations and AI camera alerts keeping everyone safe.

Our drill rigs are constantly on the move, unlocking critical resource insights across our mine sites and tenements.

#BHP #Mining #AI 

📸 Pictured is the Caval leadership team with Ausdrill representatives in front of Rig 36.

"As we have celebrated BHP’s 140-year anniversary this year, one constant has been clear: our story has always been defined by the pioneering spirit and strength of our people. From the trades of the past to the global teams of today and the innovators of tomorrow, our greatest asset will always be our extraordinary people." - Jad Vodopija, Chief People Officer, BHP

The quality of our resources, our choice of commodities, and the strength of our balance sheet offer resilience and reliability in an uncertain world. 

But it’s the character of our people that will continue to make all the difference. 

Learn more 👉 https://bhp.co/QZ

#BHP140 #BHP #People

Did you know BHP has its own awards night? It’s called the Health, Safety, Environment and Community (HSEC) Awards, and it’s held every year to recognise excellence and outstanding achievements by our people across the business.

Meet Kerry – an occupational therapist, mum of one, and Superintendent of our Maintenance Redesign Team – who won an HSEC Award in the Health and Hygiene category. Kerry played a key role in leading her team to reduce musculoskeletal injuries through smarter maintenance design.

For Kerry, the best part of her job is the people she gets to work with – and that’s why she’s so passionate about preventing injuries and improving workplaces.

Congratulations Kerry and team for your exceptional work!

Meet Sofía and Camila, welders at our Spence operations in Chile. 

They are participants in our Trainee Program, which offers tailored training in mining skills for individuals with no prior experience or background, enabling them to successfully enter the mining industry. 

Check out their testimonials. 👇 

#Spence #BHP #Mining

2025 was a year of progress and possibility at BHP!

Here’s a snapshot of the milestones and achievements that defined our journey - we’re proud of what we’ve accomplished together.

Thank you to our incredible team and partners for making it happen. Wishing you a safe and happy holiday season - see you in 2026! 

#BHPWrapped #ThinkBig #BHP140
